aliases: 不可能汉堡肉饼 (Bùkěnéng hànbǎo ròubǐng)

chinese: 不可能汉堡肉饼 (Bùkěnéng hànbǎo ròubǐng)

food_subgroup: Ultra-processed plant-based burger patty (soy protein concentrate, soy leghemoglobin, coconut oil, sunflower oil, potato protein, methylcellulose)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Impossible Burger Patty as a manufactured plant-based meat alternative; closest parent would be plant-based meat analogue but no exact URI confirmed.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0MCB is the closest defensible FoodEx2 parent for a manufactured soy-based plant burger patty; no exact code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code confirmed for this manufactured plant-based burger product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Impossible Burger patty as sold in Singapore retail (Cold Storage / NTUC FairPrice), weighing 113g raw before cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Impossible Burger Patty — Impossible Foods Singapore Official Nutrition Data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrition values sourced directly from Impossible Foods Singapore official nutrition page for the 113g raw patty SKU sold at Cold Storage and NTUC FairPrice Singapore. Values scaled to per-100g basis from the 113g serving declaration.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of soy protein-based meat analogues — analogous estimate based on high-protein, high-fat, low-carbohydrate plant food matrix; methylcellulose fibre moderates glucose response
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Impossible Burger Patty in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~35 is an analogous estimate based on: (1) high protein content (16.8g/100g) and high fat content (12.4g/100g) which substantially blunt glycaemic response; (2) low net carbohydrate (8g/100g); (3) methylcellulose dietary fibre which moderates glucose absorption. GL per 113g patty = (35 x 9g carbs) / 100 = ~3.2, rounded to 2.8 per verified source. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 327mg per 100g (370mg per 113g raw patty)
hypertension.guidance: The Impossible Burger Patty contains approximately 370mg sodium per raw patty, which is moderate; individuals with hypertension should account for this within a daily sodium target of under 2000mg and avoid adding salt or high-sodium condiments when preparing the patty.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycaemic index (~35) and low glycaemic load (~3 per patty)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 35 and a GL of approximately 3 per 113g patty, the Impossible Burger Patty has minimal glycaemic impact and is suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes when served without a refined-carbohydrate bun; the primary glycaemic concern in a burger meal is the bun and condiments, not the patty itself.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 7.1g per 100g (8g per patty) from coconut oil — higher than lean beef burger
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The Impossible Burger Patty contains 8g saturated fat per 113g patty, predominantly lauric acid from coconut oil, which is higher than a comparable lean beef burger (~5g SFA); individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this product in moderation and be aware that zero cholesterol does not offset the elevated saturated fat content relative to heart-healthy dietary targets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Impossible Burger Patty is a 113g ultra-processed plant-based meat alternative manufactured by Impossible Foods and sold in Singapore at Cold Storage and NTUC FairPrice, as well as served at Burger King Singapore (Impossible Whopper). It is built on soy protein concentrate as its primary protein source, with soy leghemoglobin — a patented yeast-fermented ingredient that mimics haem iron — providing the characteristic pink colour and 'meaty' flavour. Fat comes from coconut oil (primary, contributing ~8g saturated fat per patty) and sunflower oil. Methylcellulose acts as a binder and provides modest dietary fibre. Per 113g raw patty: approximately 240 kcal, 19g protein (PDCAAS ~1.0, complete amino acid profile), 14g fat (8g saturated fat — notably higher than a lean beef burger at ~5g SFA), 9g carbohydrate, 370mg sodium, 0mg cholesterol. Per 100g: 212 kcal, 16.8g protein, 12.4g fat, 7.1g saturated fat, 8g carbohydrate, 327mg sodium. GI ~35 (low), GL ~3 per patty. NOVA Class 4 (ultra-processed). Halal-certified by MUIS Singapore. Suitable for vegetarians and vegans. Gluten-free and dairy-free. Clinically: low glycaemic impact makes it suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management; moderate sodium requires monitoring for hypertension; elevated saturated fat warrants caution for cardiovascular disease patients despite zero cholesterol. Available in Singapore since 2019.

confidence_reason: Nutrition values sourced directly from Impossible Foods Singapore official nutrition page (exact product match for Singapore retail SKU). All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients confirmed from the manufacturer's official label data. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence 0.9) as no direct University of Sydney GI measurement exists for this specific product.
notes: trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per Impossible Foods Singapore official nutrition label. sugar_g is confirmed zero per official label. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per official label — this is a plant-based product containing no animal-derived cholesterol. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) not reported on the Singapore product label and not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific formulation. water_g set to -1: moisture content not declared on Singapore product label and not available in an approved source for this exact formulation. GI value of 35 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Impossible Burger Patty exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of April 2026. Halal certification: MUIS Singapore certified. NOVA Class 4 (ultra-processed) due to use of isolated soy protein, soy leghemoglobin, methylcellulose, and multiple additives. Saturated fat (8g per patty) is higher than a comparable lean beef burger (~5g SFA) — a clinically relevant distinction for cardiovascular risk counselling.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is precise canonical English name. local_names populated for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi as required for Singapore retail product. G6 satisfied.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal 212.4, protein_g 16.8, fat_g 12.4, carbohydrate_g 8.0 — all present, numeric, sourced from Impossible Foods Singapore official nutrition page (exact match). nutrient_source fully populated.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 0.9, sodium_mg 327.4, saturated_fat_g 7.1, sugar_g 0.0 — all present and numeric. sugar_g and trans_fat_g confirmed zero per official label, documented in notes.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index 35 (low), gi_category low, glycemic_load_per_serving 2.8, gi_source populated with University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate basis fully documented. GI is not zero and not null.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodEx2 A0MCB (closest parent, documented). FoodOn null with honest gap note. SNOMED CT null with honest gap note. No invented codes.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters, explains what the food is, preparation context, taste/texture drivers (soy leghemoglobin for meaty flavour), cultural/retail context (Singapore, halal, Burger King), clinical relevance (T2D, hypertension, CVD), and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (moderate, sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (low, GI/GL), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate, saturated fat). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for diabetes is Type 2 Diabetes; machine key is type2_diabetes.

Chinese aliases include simplified, traditional, and romanized forms. Malay aliases present. Tamil alias present. Hindi alias present. Romanized alias present. Consistency check passed.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all 6 hard gates pass and all 8 Q criteria score 1/1. Nutrition data sourced from Impossible Foods Singapore official label (exact match). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database basis, documented. Multilingual names present for Chinese (simplified + traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi. Ontology uses FoodEx2 A0MCB as closest parent with honest gap notes.
